title: Vriesea longicaulis
taxonomy: Vriesea longicaulis is a species of flowering plant in the family Bromeliaceae. This genus, Vriesea, is a group of epiphytic, terrestrial, or epilithic plants native to the Americas. The species Vriesea longicaulis is further classified as belonging to the subfamily Tillandsioideae, which includes many other species of bromeliads. The classification of Vriesea longicaulis reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history as a member of the Bromeliaceae family. As a result, Vriesea longicaulis is classified as Vriesea longicaulis (syn. Nidularium longicaulis) (Tillandsioideae: Bromeliaceae).

intro: Vriesea longicaulis, commonly known as the 'Tiger's Tail' or 'Long-tail Air Plant,' is a species of epiphytic bromeliad native to the tropical forests of South America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities, which may aid in the treatment of wounds, respiratory infections, and gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Vriesea longicaulis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Vriesea longicaulis is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in topical creams and ointments to promote wound healing and skin health.
